title: These two founders left Goldman and Meta to build voice AI for markets everyone else overlooked
summary: The startup's own stack for Africa and Middle East is now handling more than 17,000 calls per day.

title: GitLab cuts 14% of staff as it scales its platform to serve AI workloads
summary: The company is reducing its workforce as it exits 22 countries, reduces management layers, and invests in its infrastructure to scale its platform.

title: Publishers will be able to opt out of AI Search, thanks to new regulation
summary: U.K. regulators are requiring Google offer a tool allowing website publishers to opt-out of generative AI search features. The option will be tested in the UK then rolled out globally.

title: TikTok launches TikTok Pro Events, an app for cultural moments like the FIFA World Cup
summary: The app allows users to engage with other fans, explore trending videos, and access curated creator feeds.

title: The worst hacks and breaches of 2026 (so far)
summary: From a massive DOGE data breach and the hacking of critical energy and water systems to the hack of an FBI surveillance system, here are the most damaging security incidents and data breaches of 2026.

title: Meet Wander, a StumbleUpon-inspired tool for discovering the ‘small web’
summary: This open-source community project lets you create a StumbleUpon-like experience for recommending your favorite sites.

title: Meta’s AI agent for WhatsApp Business is now available globally
summary: WhatsApp will charge businesses for using its AI agent based on token usage

title: Coralogix raises $200M on bet that someone needs to watch the AI agents
summary: The Series F round values Coralogix at $1.6 billion and comes less than a year after its previous raise.

title: Plex adds new social features ahead of a major price hike for its lifetime pass
summary: Plex has come a long way from being just a personal media server. Over the past few years, it has transformed into a streaming hub, today featuring ad-supported content and movie rental options. Now, the company is setting its sights on competing with social networking platforms like Reddit and Letterboxd: on Wednesday, Plex unveiled several […]

title: The world’s largest privately owned laser just turned on
summary: Fusion startup Xcimer fired up the world's largest privately owned laser.

title: Squishmallows, dentures, and an ‘I Heart Hot Dads’ bag: Uber has found thousands of items left in robotaxis
summary: Even in a future of robot taxis, someone still has to return the things passengers leave behind.

title: Cyera eyes $12B valuation at 80x ARR multiple despite operating losses
summary: The cybersecurity company is nearing a $300 million round led by Evolution Equity Partners.

title: Cyberdecks are having a moment, rejecting big tech surveillance with style and substance
summary: Over the last few months, these DIY hardware communities have exploded in popularity as people on social media show off their solar-powered game emulators, pocket-sized ereaders, and clamshell purse computers.

title: Uber caps employee AI spending after blowing through budget in 4 months
summary: Uber's cutback has occurred after the company had reportedly encouraged staff to use AI as much as possible.

title: New Microsoft tool lets devs spin up AI behavior tests using text descriptions
summary: Microsoft on Tuesday took the wraps off Adaptive Spec-driven Scoring for Evaluation and Regression Testing, an open source framework for spinning up AI evaluations.

title: A startup, Everand, is now bundling e-books, audiobooks, and book clubs in challenge to Amazon
summary: A new reading subscription from Everand offers access to both e-books and audiobooks, and Fable's book club community.

title: Martin Scorsese becomes the latest — and most unlikely — Hollywood voice for AI
summary: The caveat is that one of the world's most famous living directors is using the tech solely for storyboarding.

title: Microsoft launches Scout, an OpenClaw-inspired personal assistant
summary: Launched at Build, Microsoft Scout is a new AI assistant meant to bring the power and flexibility of OpenClaw into the Microsoft 365 system.

title: Ex-Anduril engineer raises $42M to build the Amazon of composite parts
summary: Layup Parts co-founder Zack Eakin has drawn on a motorsports background, and his experience working for Palmer Luckey and Elon Musk, to tackle making faster, cheaper, and better composites.

title: Google rolls out fake call detection to protect against AI deepfake impersonation scams
summary: As people increasingly refuse to answer calls from unknown numbers, scammers are shifting their tactics by spoofing trusted phone numbers and using AI deepfake technology to sound like authority figures, family members, or employers.
